title: Complete fresh-host Codex audit delivery with release identity
action: Adopt the focused green implementation from dropped cell 4: ensure bee-codex-subagent-audit.mjs is in the canonical onboarding payload and fresh-host tests prove every generated Codex handler reference resolves to a copied file. Regenerate the canonical release manifest for the changed onboarding source/test, then prove onboarding, hook contracts, release identity, and diff checks.

truths: Fresh-host Codex repo-copy onboarding copies every handler referenced by generated hooks.
Both SubagentStart and SubagentStop resolve to the copied bounded audit handler.
Plugin-first suppression and Claude projection behavior remain unchanged.
The canonical release manifest contains the final hashes and passes its check.

artifacts.substantive: canonical hook payload includes the Codex audit handler
canonical release identity regenerated for final sources

key_links: generated Codex hook commands resolve only to files in the onboarding copied hook payload
release manifest hashes the exact final onboarding source and tests
prohibitions: Do not hand-edit protected .codex or .agents projections.
Do not change Claude hook topology.
Do not weaken release checks or run review.

verify: node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs && node hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs && node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --check && git diff --check

trace.outcome: Fresh-host Codex repo-copy onboarding ships every generated handler, including paired subagent audit delivery, with refreshed release identity.
